title: [Process Doc Update]: Create README for OctoAcme Project Management Docs with links to all docs and project management processes summary
body:
## Which process document do you want to update?
**<new document>**

## Summary of New Content
Create a comprehensive README for the OctoAcme Project Management Documentation that serves as the central entry point for all process documentation. This README should:

- Provide an overview of OctoAcme's project management approach
- Include organized links to all existing process documents in the docs/ folder
- Serve as a navigation hub for team members accessing project management processes

## Why is this update needed?
Currently, the docs/ folder contains multiple process documents but lacks a centralized README to guide users. This creates friction for team members trying to understand:
- What documentation exists
- How the different processes interconnect
- Which document to reference for specific scenarios

A comprehensive README will:
- Reduce onboarding friction for new team members
- Improve discoverability of process documents
- Serve as a quick reference for the overall project management methodology
- Align with the Copilot Spaces initiative to centralize institutional knowledge

## Suggested Content

### README Structure
```markdown
# OctoAcme Project Management Documentation

## Welcome
This documentation hub contains all the process guides, templates, and best practices used by OctoAcme to run successful projects.

## Project Management Overview
OctoAcme uses a customer-first, iterative approach to project delivery with clear ownership, data-informed decisions, and psychological safety. Our methodology emphasizes:
- Customer value and usability
- Small, testable increments
- Named accountability (Project Manager & Product Lead per project)
- Evidence-based iteration
- Collaborative feedback culture

## Documentation Links

### Core Foundation
- [Project Management Overview](./octoacme-project-management-overview.md) - High-level principles, roles, and lifecycle overview
- [Roles and Personas](./octoacme-roles-and-personas.md) - Detailed descriptions of team members and their responsibilities

### Project Lifecycle Phases
- [Project Initiation](./octoacme-project-initiation.md) - Starting new projects: problem statements, stakeholders, timeline
- [Project Planning](./octoacme-project-planning.md) - Detailed planning: scope, resources, milestones, dependencies
- [Execution and Tracking](./octoacme-execution-and-tracking.md) - Day-to-day delivery: standups, progress tracking, quality assurance
- [Risks and Communication](./octoacme-risks-and-communication.md) - Risk management and stakeholder communication
- [Release and Deployment](./octoacme-release-and-deployment.md) - Launch preparation, deployment, and verification
- [Retrospective and Continuous Improvement](./octoacme-retrospective-and-continuous-improvement.md) - Post-project learning and process improvements

## Getting Started
1. Read the Project Management Overview for foundational understanding
2. Review Roles and Personas to understand team structure
3. Follow the relevant lifecycle documents based on your project phase
4. Consult specific process documents as needed

## Contributing to These Docs
To suggest updates, clarifications, or new content:
- Use the [Add Content to Project Management Process Docs](../.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/add-update-content-to-process-docs.yml) issue template
- Share your feedback during retrospectives
- Collaborate with the Project Management Office

## Questions?
Refer to the relevant process document or contact your Project Manager for clarification.
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] Content aligns with existing process docs
- [x] Update improves clarity or closes a documented gap
- [x] Proposed content has been reviewed with stakeholders (if needed)
